data.title: LLM Behavioral Debugger Sub-Agent (bot-psychologist) with fix-bug Routing
data.description:
## Context

Practitioners maintaining DSO plugin skills, agents, and prompt templates encounter bugs where an LLM agent behaves incorrectly — skipping workflow steps, misinterpreting instructions, producing wrong output formats, or drifting from defined behavior. Today, /dso:fix-bug routes these to generic code-investigation agents that apply five-whys analysis and code tracing — techniques designed for executable code defects, not prompt/instruction failures. The result: behavioral bugs are misdiagnosed or produce ineffective fixes requiring multiple investigation-fix cycles where one targeted diagnosis should suffice.

## Research Artifacts

The following files contain the research foundation for the bot-psychologist agent and MUST be provided as context to all stories and implementation tasks that write or modify the agent definition:

- llm-debugging-agent.md (commit 4af34287) — Draft agent prompt with 15-point failure taxonomy, experimental probe toolkit, execution loop, KERNEL framework, and output format. Access via: git show 4af34287:llm-debugging-agent.md
- llm-debugging-research.md (commit 4af34287) — Four research loops: prompt engineering patterns, token optimization (20% Rule), failure mode taxonomy (15 weighted modes), and RCA frameworks (Gold Context Test, differential diagnosis probes, EDP antidotes). Access via: git show 4af34287:llm-debugging-research.md
- plugins/dso/docs/archive/debugging-research-2026-03-18.md — Academic research synthesis: investigation-before-fix discipline, bug reproduction, TDD, root cause vs symptom, plausible-vs-correct patches, structured reasoning, multi-agent convergence, escalation criteria, bug classification.

Additional research context (fills gaps in existing docs):
- MAST taxonomy (arxiv 2503.13657) — 14 multi-agent failure modes: specification/design (41.8%), inter-agent misalignment (36.9%), verification (21.3%). Relevant because DSO uses multi-agent orchestration; existing 15-point taxonomy covers single-agent failures but not cross-agent coordination failures.
- AgentDebug (arxiv 2509.25370) — Decomposes trajectories into decision steps, isolates minimal root-cause failures, provides corrective feedback. +24% accuracy, +26% task success.
- AGENTSCOPE (OpenReview iHU4LYSgTD) — Neuro-symbolic behavioral abstractions for agent failure diagnosis: both localization and attribution.
- AgentRx (Microsoft Research, March 2026) — Constraint synthesis from tool schemas + guarded step-by-step evaluation. +23.6% failure localization, +22.9% root-cause attribution.

## Success Criteria

1. Fix-bug Step 1 gains an llm-behavioral classification branch: when ticket content references agent/skill names or prompt behavior, OR when all affected files are .md skill/agent/prompt files, the bug is classified as llm-behavioral and routed to dso:bot-psychologist (either signal alone is sufficient)
2. A dso:bot-psychologist agent definition exists at plugins/dso/agents/bot-psychologist.md with the 15-point failure taxonomy, RCA probe definitions (Gold Context Test, Closed-Book Test, Prompt Perturbation, Sycophancy Probe, State-Check Probe), and KERNEL minimal-fix constraint — all defined inline in the agent file
3. The bot-psychologist report uses fix-bug RESULT schema fields (ROOT_CAUSE, confidence, proposed_fixes, hypothesis_tests with verdict) so Step 3.5 Hypothesis Validation Gate works without modification — proposed_fixes includes the combined fix proposal
4. Fix-bug llm-behavioral path skips Step 5 (RED test) and Step 5.5 (RED-before-fix gate) since .md behavioral fixes have no executable RED test; Step 3.5 and Step 4 still apply
5. Fix-bug HARD-GATE is amended to allow combined investigation + fix-proposal for llm-behavioral bugs, where the two are a single cognitive task
6. After all other success criteria are met and verified, the two root-level research files (llm-debugging-agent.md and llm-debugging-research.md from commit 4af34287) are deleted from the repository — their content has been absorbed into the agent definition and the archive doc remains as the canonical research reference

## Scenario Analysis

- False-negative: mixed-file bugs route to wrong agent (high) — keyword signal independent of file type mitigates
- False-positive: agent name keywords in code bug tickets (high) — require behavioral language not just name mentions
- Nested sub-agent prohibition (high) — read agent definition inline when running as sub-agent (same as complexity-evaluator)
- Step 3.5 schema incompatibility (high) — SC3 mandates existing hypothesis_tests/verdict fields
- RED test gate blocks .md-only fixes (high) — SC4 explicitly exempts llm-behavioral path
- agent-routing.conf and naming consistency (medium) — must update routing conf with llm_behavioral category
- Tests requiring live LLM block CI (medium) — use structural assertions not live LLM calls

## Dependencies

Fix-bug HARD-GATE amendment is an in-scope change (SC5), not an external dependency. No external epic dependencies.

## Approach

Create plugins/dso/agents/bot-psychologist.md with the 15-point failure taxonomy (from existing research), MAST inter-agent failure modes, experimental probe toolkit, and KERNEL minimal-fix discipline. Add llm-behavioral classification to fix-bug Step 1 with dual-signal detection (ticket content + file type). Amend fix-bug HARD-GATE and gate exemptions for the new path. Update agent-routing.conf. Follow existing sub-agent patterns (SUB-AGENT-GUARD, inline read when nested).

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: novel architectural pattern
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 11
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: web research re-run 1 time(s)
